title: RISC-V Studio
description: A complete RV32IMAFC + Zicsr assembler, cycle-stepping emulator, and time-travel debugger in the browser — with the compressed (C) extension, a three-ring privilege architecture (M/S/U) and a real hardware Sv32 MMU (two-level page-table walker, V/R/W/X/U/G/A/D bits, TLB, page faults, trap delegation). NEWEST: a microarchitecture PERFORMANCE LAB. A trace-driven timing model layers a classic 5-stage in-order pipeline (IF/ID/EX/MEM/WB) over the unchanged interpreter's retired-instruction stream — so functional results stay byte-for-byte identical while you see how fast real hardware would run the program. It models data hazards with optional data FORWARDING (and the unavoidable one-cycle load-use bubble), no-forwarding write-before-read stalls, multi-cycle EX latencies (mul/div, fp add/mul/div), configurable BRANCH PREDICTORS (static, 1-bit, 2-bit bimodal, gshare) over a BTB with a side-by-side accuracy comparison, and a set-associative I-cache + D-cache hierarchy (size/block/associativity/LRU-FIFO/write-back-through). It reports total cycles, CPI/IPC, a where-the-cycles-go stall breakdown, cache hit/miss tables, and the textbook instruction × cycle PIPELINE DIAGRAM with stalls and mispredictions flagged — every knob re-running the model live, and the cycle counts proven against hand-computed oracles in the in-app suite. All built on the existing machine-mode trap & interrupt architecture (mtvec/mepc/mcause, mret, a CLINT timer), a from-scratch C compiler that lowers a real subset of C to RISC-V, IEEE-754 float, atomics, CSR counters, a memory-mapped framebuffer, syscall console, disassembler, shareable links, and a 75-check in-app verification suite — all strictly additive, so with paging off the machine is byte-for-byte the bare-metal RV32 it always was.
